content: We can frame Self-Refine's iterative un-supervised output improvement framework as a structural 'skeleton' that can be instantiated with multiple inconsistent self-perspectives borrowed from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a hybrid self-improvement system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no extra training or supervision while boosting refinement accuracy. This hybrid approach addresses the key limitation of vanilla Self-Refine's single-perspective feedback blind spots by leveraging self-contrast from divergent solving perspectives.
